subject: Chemistry
title: Rocket Fuel Chemistry
description: A short lesson to help you apply Rocket Fuel Chemistry.

hook.text: The Saturn V rocket burned 15 tons of fuel per second to escape Earth's gravity.

buildup.text: Rockets carry both fuel and oxidizer because there's no oxygen in space to support combustion.

discovery.text: Liquid hydrogen and liquid oxygen combine in a controlled explosion. The exhaust is superheated steam.

twist.text: Some rockets use solid fuel that can't be turned off once ignited. You're committed the moment it lights.

climax.text: SpaceX's Raptor uses methane and oxygen—cheaper, reusable, and powerful enough for Mars.

punchline.text: Controlled explosions, aimed downward. That's how rockets work.

quiz.question: Why do rockets carry their own oxidizer?
options: There is no oxygen in space to support combustion
It prevents the fuel from freezing
